mnemonics: CRUD-L (Create, Read, Update, Delete, Link) - The core operations you can perform on a Singly Linked List.
keyPoints: Traversal requires starting at the head and following next pointers until null.
Insertion and deletion at the head are O(1) operations.
Insertion and deletion at a specific node require traversal to find the previous node, making it an O(n) operation.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to update the 'next' pointer of the previous node when deleting, causing a broken list.
Losing the reference to the head of the list when performing an insertion or deletion at the beginning.
Incorrectly handling edge cases like an empty list or a list with only one node.
